name: Khawli ibn Yazid
title: Carrier of the Imam's Head
shortDescription: One of Kufa's criminals who took Imam Hussain's (AS) holy head and hid it inside the oven in his house in Kufa.
fullStory:
Khawli ibn Yazid al-Asbahi was a minor and hard-hearted commander in the Kufan army who held a deep grudge against the Ahl al-Bayt (AS). During Ashura, he played a prominent role in looting the tents and disrespecting the martyrs' bodies.

After Imam Hussain (AS) fell in the slaughter pit, Khawli advanced along with Sinan ibn Anas to sever the Imam's head. When Khawli got close, a severe tremor overtook his body and he couldn't do it, so Sinan (or Shimr) severed the Imam's head and gave it to Khawli.

Umar ibn Sa'd entrusted the holy head to Khawli to quickly take it to Kufa at night and bring the glad tidings to Ibn Ziyad. Khawli reached Kufa at night, but the palace doors were closed. He took the head home and, fearing his wife (Nawar, who loved the Ahl al-Bayt), hid it inside the house's oven.

That night, his wife saw a light ascending from the oven to the sky and separated from Khawli from that moment. Khawli was eventually captured by Mukhtar's forces. Mukhtar killed him in his own house and burned his body.
quoteArabic: جِئْتُكِ بِغِنى الدَّهْرِ... هذا رَأْسُ الحُسَيْنِ مَعِي فِي الدّارِ!
quote: (He told his wife:) I have brought you the wealth of the ages... This is Hussain's head with me in the house!
